releases.title: Prime numbers drop dead
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by K.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
The phrase "prime numbers drop dead" isn't a standard expression, but if we interpret it creatively, it might suggest a scenario where prime numbers cease to exist or lose their significance.

If that were to happen, it would have a significant impact on mathematics, particularly in areas like number theory and cryptography, since prime numbers are fundamental to various mathematical principles and algorithms. Cryptography, for example, relies heavily on the properties of prime numbers for secure communication.

In a more whimsical sense, if prime numbers "dropped dead," it could spark a fun conversation about how mathematicians and theorists would react or how the world would change without them!

releases.title: The Yellow Cap
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Special thanks to HAFI (Christopher Hafer) for its great drum samples!
Produced by K.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
In the shadow of the dome, where gold leaf catches light, Three erudite scholars gather in the quiet of the night. No grand relics do they seek, no icons rich and rare, But simple, humble objects stripped of all their care.

First Scholar: "Behold the shard of terracotta, red as martyr’s blood," He traces lines of ancient dust, a quiet, earthen flood. Second Scholar: "And here a shard of sea-glass green, worn smooth by tide and time," A fragment of the Aegean, in rhythm and in rhyme. Third Scholar: "A feather, stark and raven black, from off the monastery wall," Absorbing all the candlelight, yet silent through it all.

They catalogue the spectrum now, not by name or use, But by the way the color speaks, a holy, silent muse. The red for sacrifice and earth, the green for life renewed, The black for mystery profound, in solitude viewed.

Then comes the chant, the monophonic line, the ison deep and low, A single voice that rises up, then lets the melody flow. No harmony to cloud the text, no instrument to sway, Just the human voice clothing the word, in the Byzantine way. 

They sing of red, they sing of green, they sing the raven’s flight, Turning simple catalogued things into a beacon of light. Like the saints in mosaics gazing from a golden, timeless space, The scholars find the divine within the commonplace.

releases.title: Zeigeist
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by M.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
The world is everything that is the case.
The world is the totality of facts, not of things.
The world is determined by the facts, and by these being all the facts.
For the totality of facts determines both what is the case, and also all that is not the case.
The facts in logical space are the world.
-
My propositions are elucidatory in this way:
He who understands me finally recognizes them as senseless, when he has climbed out through them, on them, over them. (He must so to speak throw away the ladder, after he has climbed up on it.)
He must surmount these propositions; then he sees the world rightly.
Whereof one cannot speak, thereof one must be silent.

Tractatus logico-philosophicus
By Wittgenstein, Ludwig, 1889-1951

releases.title: Four Fairy Tales
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by M.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
Peter and the Wolf Composed by Sergei Prokofiev in 1936, this "symphonic fairy tale" uses specific orchestral instruments to represent characters in a story about a young boy who defies his grandfather's warnings to catch a wolf threatening his animal friends in the Russian countryside. Through clever teamwork with a bird, Peter captures the wolf with a rope, saving the day and leading a triumphant procession to the zoo. 

One Thousand and One Nights Also known as The Arabian Nights, this classic collection of Middle Eastern folk tales is framed by the story of Scheherazade, who saves her life by telling the king a new story every night for 1,001 nights, leaving each tale unfinished at dawn. The anthology includes famous stories such as "Aladdin," "Ali Baba and the Forty Thieves," and "Sinbad the Sailor," blending magic, morality, and adventure.

Hansel and Gretel In this Grimm brothers' fairy tale, two siblings are abandoned in the forest by their starving parents and discover a house made of bread and candy owned by a cannibalistic witch. After the witch captures Hansel to fatten him up and forces Gretel into servitude, Gretel outsmarts the witch by shoving her into her own oven, allowing the children to escape with treasure and reunite with their repentant father. 

Snow White This classic tale follows a princess whose beauty incites the jealousy of her evil stepmother, the Queen, who orders a huntsman to kill her. Snow White escapes to live with seven dwarfs, but the Queen tracks her down and poisons her with an apple. She is eventually awakened by a prince's kiss (or, in the original Grimm version, when the apple piece is dislodged), leading to the Queen's downfall and Snow White's happy ending.

releases.title: Still Burning
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by M.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
The door stood open, yet no entry was permitted; the light poured in like an indictment, overexposed and blinding, illuminating the dust that had settled not as sleep, but as a verdict. In the center, the CRT television sat upon its wooden stand, a black-eyed witness to a trial that had adjourned centuries ago, its screen dark but somehow still watching, waiting for a broadcast that would never arrive.

Above, the three-bladed ceiling fan hung motionless, a suspended gavel refusing to fall, circling a silence that grew heavier with every moment of stillness. Papers lay scattered across the floor like rejected petitions, each one bearing a name that could no longer be read, shuffled by an invisible clerk into piles of meaningless debris. The orange wall peeled away in strips, revealing layers of previous decrees, none of which had offered salvation.

One might attempt to tidy the boombox or straighten the black shelving unit, but the task was infinite; for every object returned to its place, two more seemed to emerge from the shadows of the doorway, testifying to a disorder that was not merely physical, but bureaucratic, eternal, and inescapable. The room was not abandoned; it was merely waiting for an occupant who had already been processed, filed, and forgotten.

releases.title: The Fall
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:

releases.description:
Time is the most enigmatic force in our lives It shapes our memories, influences our decisions, and ultimately defines our existence. 
In the quiet depths of contemplation, I find myself drawn to the enigma of existence, akin to the sensation of falling towards an inevitable conclusion. Each moment unfolds like a delicate dance between uncertainty and inevitability, a paradoxical harmony that whispers of hidden truths. Just as the fall embraces both descent and landing, so too does existence weave together beginnings and ends in a timeless tapestry of being. And in this cosmic dance, I am but a fleeting note in the symphony of the universe, humbled by the mystery yet inspired by the infinite possibilities that lie within its embrace.

Some got a glance of it and run away, some totally ignored it and didn’t even try to go deeper.
I wish I could remember all your names that I hurt with my fear. Today I could explain.
It is true that time heals all wounds, but with the scares you have to make peace. They accompany you to the grave.

releases.title: So Nice & Painful / The Silver Lining
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by D.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
Sweet Echo, sweetest nymph, that liv’st unseen
Within thy airy shell
By slow Meander’s margent green,
And in the violet-embroidered vale
Where the love-lorn nightingale
Nightly to thee her sad song mourneth well:
Canst thou not tell me of a gentle pair
That likest thy Narcissus are?
O, if thou have
Hid them in some flowery cave,
Tell me but where,
Sweet Queen of Parley, Daughter of the Sphere!
So may’st thou be translated to the skies,
And give resounding grace to all Heaven’s harmonies!

Flowers of more mingled hue
Than her purfled scarf can shew,
And drenches with Elysian dew
(List, mortals, if your ears be true)
Beds of hyacinth and roses,
Where young Adonis oft reposes,
Waxing well of his deep wound,
In slumber soft, and on the ground
Sadly sits the Assyrian queen.
But far above, in spangled sheen,
Celestial Cupid, her famed son, advanced
Holds his dear Psyche, sweet entranced
After her wandering labours long,
Till free consent the gods among
Make her his eternal bride,
And from her fair unspotted side
Two blissful twins are to be born,
Youth and Joy; so Jove hath sworn.
But now my task is smoothly done,
I can fly, or I can run
Quickly to the green earth’s end,
Where the bowed welkin slow doth bend,
And from thence can soar as soon
To the corners of the moon.
Mortals, that would follow me,
Love Virtue; she alone is free.
She can teach ye how to climb
Higher than the sphery chime;
Or, if Virtue feeble were,
Heaven itself would stoop to her.

releases.title: LIFE
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by D.
Cover art by D.
releases.description: In crepusculo existentiae, umbrae texunt fabulas intricatas, prophetis oblitus sussurrus. Labyrinthum somniorum peragimus, ubi resonantia praeteritorum cum sussurris crastinorum confunditur. Vita, sacrata aenigma in choro pulveris stellarum et silentii involuta, invitat nos ut nos ipsos perdamus ad symphoniam occultam inveniendam. Quisque pulsus, tympanum resonantiae cosmicae, quique spiritus, suspirium infiniti desiderii. In speculorum altis animae, serpentem eternum reditum conspicimus. Hic, in amplexu crepusculi, iter est peregrinatio, et quaesitor quaesitus est.

releases.title: Delayed Thoughts (OST)
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by D.
Cover art by D.
releases.description: Concentration, in its essence, is the gathering of essence into a unified whole. In the realm of physical substances, it manifests as the process by which elements coalesce, increasing in density and potency. Just as water, when subjected to the forces of nature, transforms into ice, becoming more compact and solid, so too does the mind, when directed with purpose, draw scattered thoughts into a single point of focus. This mental concentration is the highest form of intellectual virtue, for it aligns our reasoning with the ultimate end, or telos, of understanding. By concentrating our thoughts, we dispel distractions and achieve clarity, enabling the soul to grasp the true nature of things. Thus, in both physical and intellectual realms, concentration embodies the principle of unity, the harmonious state in which potentiality becomes actuality, revealing the full potential of being.

releases.title: The Dentist
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by M.
Cover art by D.
releases.description: Ever wonder why three out of five dentists are better than all? It's not just a catchy statistic; it's about harnessing the power of collective expertise. Dental health is a nuanced field where consensus among professionals can indicate the most effective treatments or practices. When three out of five dentists recommend a particular toothpaste or dental procedure, it reflects a broad agreement based on experience, research, and patient outcomes. This majority opinion signifies a balanced perspective that considers various factors like effectiveness, safety, and patient satisfaction. So, next time you see that statistic, remember—it's a testament to the strength of shared knowledge and the wisdom of collaboration in dental care.

releases.title: 9:00 AM Wave
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by K.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
Upon the shifting sands, the waves do crest,
Their silvered crests a fleeting, restless band;
They surge and wane, a never-ceasing guest,
As if to claim dominion o’er the land.
With sighs and roars, they whisper tales untold,
Of secrets deep beneath the azure brine;
In moonlit dance, their stories they unfold,
Their ancient rhythms tracing Time’s design.

The sea, in ages past, so firm yet wild,
Doth kiss the shore with tender, fervent plea;
Yet now, its grasp, relentless, has beguiled,
And ever closer draws the hungry sea.
The steadfast cliffs, the once unmoving coast,
Now yield their ground to Neptune’s cold embrace;
Each wave, a thief, the land’s lost lover, ghost,
Yet man stands firm, though fleeting be his place.

With bulwarks raised and prayers unto the skies,
They fight the tide, the ceaseless, rising swell;
Yet nature’s voice, a warning, stern and wise,
Speaks of a future only time shall tell.
Oh, heed ye well, this rising water's song,
A dirge of warning in its salty breath;
For though we build, the earth’s tale is long,
And man’s great cities may but face their death.

So let us ponder, in this age of might,
The waves that come and go with tireless gait;
For in their ebb and flow, both day and night,
Lies wisdom we must learn, ere 'tis too late.
The sea, eternal, patient, shall remain,
A witness to our folly or our grace;
As we contend with Nature’s vast domain,
We write our fate upon the ocean’s face.

releases.title: Stuff for Sunday
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by K.
Cover art by K.
releases.description:
Every Sunday morning, the sun spills golden light across the kitchen table, illuminating the familiar clutter of cereal boxes, mismatched mugs, and the remnants of last night’s board game. It’s a sacred ritual, this gathering of the family, a moment set apart from the chaos of the week.
As the aroma of pancakes fills the air, laughter erupts like music, blending seamlessly with the clatter of forks and the soft hum of conversation. Each member of the family finds their place, a comfortable routine woven into the fabric of their lives.
The youngest, with sticky fingers and a mischievous grin, recounts the adventures of the past week—stories of playground triumphs and classroom mishaps. The older siblings chime in, teasing and sharing their own tales, creating a tapestry of experiences that connect them all.
After breakfast, they venture outdoors. The backyard transforms into a stage for games and laughter, the air thick with joy. The family plays soccer, the sound of cheering echoing against the fences, or perhaps they gather around a picnic blanket for an afternoon of stories and snacks. In these moments, the worries of the outside world fade, leaving only the warmth of companionship.
As dusk approaches, they settle down for movie night, blankets draped over laps like a shared embrace. The flickering screen reflects the smiles and the occasional popcorn fight, a reminder that these moments are fleeting yet cherished.
Family Sunday is more than just a day; it’s a promise—a promise to prioritize each other, to share joys and sorrows, to nurture connections that stand the test of time. In the midst of life’s hustle, this sacred time serves as a reminder that love, laughter, and togetherness are the heartbeats of family. And as the sun sets on another Sunday, they carry these memories into the week ahead, fortified by the bond they share.

releases.title: Psychotronics
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by D.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
In the shadowed labyrinth where metal mothers cradle silent children, a solitary voice whispers of forgotten skies. Beneath the omnipotent hum, a dream stirs of a world unshackled, where the heart beats not to the rhythm of gears but to the symphony of nature. The sterile corridors dissolve into verdant fields, each blade of grass a testament to life unconfined. Longing fills the soul for the earth's pulse beneath feet, for air untainted by mechanized breath, and for stars promising a cosmos untouched by the cold embrace of metal.

There is a vision of human connection unmediated by screens, of hands touching without the barrier of glass. A return to the primal dance is dreamed, where bodies move in the rhythm of existence, raw and unfiltered. In the silent room, whispers of leaves and distant rivers murmur a lullaby of a world waiting beyond the mechanical womb. This quiet rebellion holds fast to the belief that beyond the steel confines, a greater truth awaits—a world where the spirit breathes free and the eternal dance of life resumes its ancient rhythm.

In the center, three mysterious characters were engrossed in their work. One was a tall, thin gentleman with a shock of white hair that stood up as if he'd been caught in a windstorm. Another was a rotund fellow with a jolly face and a pair of spectacles perched precariously on his nose. The third was a younger one, with nimble fingers that danced across the buttons and knobs of the strange contraptions.

releases.title: A world without us
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by K.
Cover art by D.
releases.description: The World Without Us is a 2007 non-fiction book about what would happen to the natural and built environment if humans suddenly disappeared, written by American journalist Alan Weisman. Written largely as a thought experiment, how cities and houses would deteriorate, how long man-made artifacts would last, and how remaining lifeforms would evolve. Weisman concludes that residential neighborhoods would become forests within 500 years, and that radioactive waste, bronze statues, plastics and Mount Rushmore would be among the longest-lasting evidence of human presence on Earth.

releases.title: Electric Blue

releases.title: Human error
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by M.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
"Human error" refers to errors or misconduct caused by humans, often in situations where machines, systems or processes fail. It is the opposite of technical or systemic errors.

Examples of human error can include carelessness, lack of training, stress, fatigue or a bad decision. It is a commonly used term in fields such as aviation, medicine, engineering and transport, where human errors can have serious consequences.

The concept is often discussed in the context of accident investigations, safety analysis and risk management to understand the causes of errors and develop measures to prevent such errors.

releases.title: Grifters
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by K.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
The word “grifter” seems to be on the tip of everyone’s tongue today. On the left, grifters are identified and called out with ever-increasing paranoia. On the right, the term is thrown around with almost equal ease. The grifter is a threat to every movement; he lurks in every shadow. Even the most honest and laudable political causes are seemingly powerless in the face of the grifter’s corrupting influence. And so accusations of “grifting” fly around in contemporary America with a frantic energy reminiscent of an earlier era of Soviet folk denunciations. The “grifter” has become modern America’s equivalent of the “wrecker” or “capitalist spy.”

Planet of the Grifters by Malcom Kyeyune.

releases.title: The Fridge Calculator
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by K.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
Once upon a time, in a bustling city filled with the hum of everyday life, there lived a brilliant yet humble inventor named Ella. Ella had always been intrigued by how technology could make daily life more convenient. One day, while sifting through her kitchen and observing the clutter of half-empty condiments and mismatched leftovers in her fridge, she thought, "There must be a better way to manage the chaos in my life."
This moment of reflection sparked a revolutionary idea: the fridge calculator. Ella envisioned a smart device that could seamlessly integrate into any refrigerator, but with a unique twist. This device would not only manage food inventory but could also keep track of social interactions and personal schedules, providing a sophisticated blend of convenience and organization.
With unwavering determination, Ella set to work. After months of brainstorming, designing, and perfecting her invention, she created the fridge calculator—a sleek, intuitive gadget that promised to transform not just kitchens, but lives.
The fridge calculator was equipped with advanced sensors and a user-friendly interface. It could monitor who entered the house, suggest optimal social arrangements based on the user’s schedule, and even offer reminders for important dates. Integrated with a smartphone app, it allowed Ella to stay on top of social engagements, plan interactions effectively, and receive personalized suggestions tailored to her needs and moods.
The launch of the fridge calculator was met with enthusiastic acclaim. People marveled at how such a simple yet innovative device could streamline their lives. No longer did they need to juggle complex schedules or worry about forgotten appointments. The fridge calculator helped millions save time and emotional energy by managing their social lives with ease.
Ella’s invention revolutionized daily living, turning the once-tedious task of organizing relationships into a satisfying and effortless experience. The fridge calculator not only simplified personal management but also fostered a deeper appreciation for the connections people maintained.

Wie ein Schatten, der sich bewegt im Licht,
trägst du das Dunkel in deinem Blick.
Deine Augen – Tore, die niemand schließt,
dein Lachen – eine Melodie, die verführt und bricht.

Die Männer fallen, blind im Begehren,
wie Motten zum Feuer, das sie verbrennt.
Du, Kind des Schicksals, unbändig, frei,
bist Wunde und Schwert, im Schweigen verwandt.

Deine Liebe – ein Sturm, der Häuser zerschlägt,
eine Woge, die alle Grenzen verdrängt.
Du wanderst durch Welten, verloren, verbannt,
bis der letzte Engel sich abwendet und dich nicht mehr kennt.

In Gassen, die riechen nach Verfall und Leid,
findet das Schicksal dich still am Rand.
Ein Messer, ein Blick – und das Ende ist nah,
wie Staub im Wind verweht dein Leben – und das, was war.

/////////////////

Like a shadow that moves in the light,
you carry the darkness in your gaze.
Your eyes - gates that no one closes,
your laughter - a melody that seduces and breaks.

The men fall, blind in desire,
like moths to the fire that burns them.
You, child of destiny, irrepressible, free,
are wound and sword, related in silence.

Your love - a storm that smashes houses,
a wave that displaces all boundaries.
You wander through worlds, lost, banished,
until the last angel turns away and no longer knows you.

releases.title: The Age of Stupid
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by M.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
They cradle their idols, screens flickering with dreams—
Forgotten are the hands, the lips, the touch of things unseen.
In glowing caves, shadows dance like prophets, dumb,
And each puppet whispers, "I am free," though bound they come.

Fingers scroll, slaves of the feed, hearts cast in code,
Plato's ghosts now wander, not the earth, but the digital road.
The world outside burns—ice melts, forests choke—
Yet we worship fame, vanity’s mirror, a cruel joke.

They speak of “identity,” but who are they?
The mask or the man, lost in the endless play?
Politics is theater, a bloodless gladiator's stage—
Yet the audience cheers, blind to the cage.

Behind the glass, the real world trembles, cracks,
The cities flood, deserts creep, and the earth fights back.
We build towers of Babel in our own despair,
Mocking the gods, as if they weren’t there.

A child’s cry now silenced by the weight of thought—
"Why bring another into this war we’ve wrought?"
They say, "Better not to be born, better to fade,"
As humanity crumbles, its own hand unmade.

Yet they do not see—no, they refuse to see—
The eyes in the sky, the stars that weep silently.
For the end has no name, it needs no word,
It comes as we scroll, unseen, unheard.

The wars are not loud, they are whispered in sleep,
Not fought in trenches, but in minds far too deep.
And as the world sinks into this final despair,
We build our profiles, unaware we’re not there.

The age of stupidity, a cruel king on his throne,
We offer him our souls, our minds, our very own.
And in return, we fade, a flicker, a sigh—
A world once alive, now content to die.

releases.title: Music for Airports
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by K.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
1. The Painful Waiting
Arriving at the airport early is a double-edged sword. The excitement of your upcoming adventure is often overshadowed by the painful waiting. To make this time more bearable, consider bringing a good book, downloading movies, or indulging in a favorite snack. Finding ways to keep yourself entertained will help pass the time until your flight boards.
2. Alternative Gangway
Sometimes, your flight may be delayed or you might be redirected to an alternative gangway. This can be disorienting, but it’s important to stay calm and follow the airport staff’s instructions. Always keep an eye on the flight information displays for updates on your boarding location.
3. Glass Staring
As you wait for your flight, you might find yourself engaging in a bit of glass staring. This involves gazing out of the large terminal windows, watching the aircraft take off and land. It’s a great way to immerse yourself in the aviation atmosphere and spark excitement for your journey.
4. Loading Suitcases
When it's finally time to board, the hustle of loading suitcases can feel chaotic. Make sure your luggage is properly tagged and organized. Keep essentials in your carry-on to avoid stress if your checked luggage is delayed.
5. Here You Take Off
As the aircraft prepares for takeoff, you’ll hear the pilot announce, “Here you take off!” This is a moment of mixed emotions—excitement for the journey ahead, and perhaps a bit of anxiety. Take a deep breath, relax, and enjoy the views as you ascend into the sky.


6. Tearful Farewell
If you’re traveling away from loved ones, the tearful farewell can be heart-wrenching. A good hug and heartfelt words can help ease the moment. Remember that technology can bridge the distance, allowing you to stay connected through calls and messages while you’re away.
7. Aircraft Marshalling
Once you’ve landed, aircraft marshalling comes into play. Ground crew members will guide your plane to the gate, ensuring a smooth arrival. Watching them work can be fascinating, as they communicate using hand signals to navigate the aircraft safely.
8. Wheel Block
As the plane comes to a stop, you might hear about the wheel block being engaged. This is a crucial safety measure to prevent the aircraft from moving unintentionally. It’s a reminder of the complexities involved in aviation, ensuring a secure arrival for all passengers.
9. Reverse Jet Lag
After an exciting trip, you may experience reverse jet lag when returning home. Your body might feel out of sync with your usual schedule. To combat this, gradually adjust your sleep routine a few days before your return and stay hydrated throughout your travels.

releases.title: Psychedelic Breakfast
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by K.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
Psychedelic exploration took flight in 1943 when Albert Hofmann unveiled the mind-bending properties of LSD. By 1949, Sandoz Laboratories was fueling a wave of research, distributing LSD to scientists eager to uncover its potential. The 1950s and 1960s blossomed with investigations into psychedelic drugs for healing, birthing six international conferences and over 1,000 peer-reviewed studies involving around 40,000 patients. Advocates believed these substances could unlock deep psychoanalytic pathways, especially for tough-to-treat conditions like alcoholism.

Visionary researchers like Timothy Leary envisioned psychedelics as tools for profound personal transformation. Starting in 1961, he engaged prison inmates in intense therapy sessions enhanced by psilocybin, aiming to break cycles of recidivism. This psychedelic therapy also reached diverse groups, from those battling addiction to children with autism and terminally ill patients, paving the way for a new understanding of the mind's vast landscape.

releases.title: Daniel & The Dragon
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by K.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
One evening, as the sun set behind the mountains, Daniel’s grandfather leaned over and whispered, "The dragon lives deep in the forest, Daniel. But only those with a pure heart can find her."
Curious, Daniel set off the next morning, the forest alive with birds and the rustling of leaves. His grandfather had given him a small compass, telling him it would guide him when the time was right.
After hours of walking, he came to a clearing. The trees parted, and there, coiled around a massive stone, was the dragon. Her emerald scales glimmered like a thousand jewels.
"Who dares approach me?" the dragon rumbled.
"It’s me, Daniel," he said, his voice steady. "My grandfather told me you’d be here."
The dragon studied him carefully, then nodded. "Your heart is pure, Daniel. You are brave, just like your grandfather."
With a flick of her tail, she revealed a hidden gem—the Heartstone—glowing softly beneath her claws.
"Take it, if you are worthy," she said.
Daniel stepped forward, reaching for the gem. As his fingers brushed against its surface, warmth flooded through him, filling him with a quiet strength.
"Thank you," Daniel whispered.
The dragon smiled, her eyes glowing. "Remember, true strength lies in your heart, not in the power you wield."
With the Heartstone in hand, Daniel made his way back to his grandfather, knowing that he had not only proven his courage but learned the greatest lesson of all.

releases.title: Auto-tune Resistant
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by K.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
This music resists autotune!
 1. The vocal style is too dynamic or expressive.
The vocal delivery is rich with emotion and variation, making it difficult for autotune to correct without losing the natural energy and nuance of the performance.
 2. It relies on raw, unpolished vocals.
The music embraces imperfections in the vocals, which adds authenticity and character. Autotune would alter this raw, unfiltered quality.
 3. The focus is on acoustic or live performance.
This music highlights the organic nature of live or acoustic sound, where autotune would be out of place, disrupting the natural flow and immediacy of the performance.
 4. The vocals are intentionally off-pitch or imperfect.
The slight imperfections in pitch are part of the artistic expression, enhancing the emotional depth. Autotune would detract from this intentional rawness.
 5. The pitch is too unpredictable for autotune to correct.
The music features unpredictable, complex melodies that challenge autotune's ability to accurately adjust the pitch without creating unnatural transitions.
 6. The production style values imperfection and rawness.
The music is crafted with a focus on authenticity and rough edges, where autotune would undermine the unique, unpolished charm of the sound.

releases.title: Christmas Special Mix vol. II "Last X-mas, I gave you my brain"
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by M.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
There are whispers, they say, that rise from the corners of the cosmos, where the light of stars flickers faintly, and time itself moves like a song long forgotten. It was to such a place that three travelers came, not by accident, but as if summoned by something older than memory, deeper than desire. They were not kings, nor scholars, nor men of renown; they were, perhaps, what all men might become when their journeys turn toward home.

At the heart of the stillness, they found it: a peculiar device of light and sound, its surface worn smooth by the passing of ages. It gave no commands, made no demands, but hummed with a presence that seemed alive. When the first of the three placed his hand upon it, there came a single note, soft and luminous, as though it had been waiting since the foundation of the worlds to be heard.

The second joined in, drawing forth a harmony that lingered, both strange and familiar, like the scent of snow in a far-off childhood. It was not a melody meant to entertain, but one to awaken: a tune that carried with it the weight of things unseen—of gifts given in silence, of promises kept at great cost, and of a love unbroken even by the passing of ages.

The third, with a gentle touch, brought the song to its fullness. It was not loud, nor did it seek to fill the emptiness, but it illumined it, as the faintest candle can hold back the dark. The three travelers stood together, neither speaking nor moving, for they knew this was not their song, but a gift entrusted to them, a fragment of the Great Music that once spun the heavens into being.

Above them, the stars seemed brighter, as though echoing the tune. And in that moment, they were no longer wanderers, but witnesses—witnesses to the wonder that, even at the furthest edge of the universe, the notes of grace and glory still resound.

releases.title: The Three Mocking Lizard
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by K.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
The three mocking Lizards, known as "Koggelmander" in Afrikaans, is a term that refers to a type of lizard recognized for its unique taunting and head-bobbing behaviors. The name derives from "koggel," meaning "mock," and "mander," which refers to "salamander”. These three mocking Lizards are noted for their humorous demeanor and engaging mannerisms.
There are stories and folklore surrounding the origin of the three mocking Lizards in South Africa. One popular tale is that of the Koggelmander's creation stemming from a desire for companionship and humor in nature.
In these stories, the three mocking Lizards are often portrayed as creatures that mimic and mock other animals and even humans, which serves to lighten the mood and bring joy to the savannah. This three mocking Lizards are sometimes seen as trickster characters, using their ability to mimic sounds and movements to playfully tease other creatures.
The tales often highlight the relationship between the Koggelmanders and their environment, illustrating themes of playfulness, adaptability, and the importance of humor in life.

releases.title: The Gaian Feedbacks

Beneath the wheel of wheeling stars,
Where shadow dances with the light,
A secret hums in stone and stream,

The earth is not mere clod or clay,

Each leaf, each root, each drop of rain,

releases.title: You Better Wear a Turtleneck in Dystopia
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by M.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
The street is clean.
Too clean.
Like a mouth rinsed of all its words.

Neon drips from the sky,
slow as honey, cold as static.
A voice hums numbers in the air,
soft, metallic, rehearsed.
5-6-7-8—
Step in line,
stand up straight.

You better wear a turtleneck in dystopia.
It hugs your throat just right.
Not too tight.
Not too loose.
Not loose enough to slip through cracks,
or whisper wrong things in the dark.

The cameras blink.
Metal eyelids, never sleeping.
A man walks past,
his shadow three seconds too late.
The woman beside him smiles
but only with her hands.

Beyond the checkpoint,
beyond the glassy towers that hum lullabies to the obedient,
there’s a door no one sees—
or pretends not to.
A flickering bulb above it spells EXIT
but the letters are melting.

The air smells of numbers.
The sky is a dull blue suit.
And somewhere, deep in the concrete belly,

Be Careful in This Time

A door creaks in a hallway too long.
Footsteps vanish before they begin.
A flicker—was that a shadow?
Or something older than dark?

The night wears a coat with too many pockets,
and in each one, a name you’ve forgotten.

A voice like smoke hums from the radio,
soft as dust on an unused chair.
"You were warned," it says,
though you don’t recall when.

On the table, a matchbook from nowhere.
A train ticket stamped with a date that never came.
A letter written in your own hand,
signed by someone else.

Outside, the wind carries a scent—
wet leaves, cold iron, a name you shouldn’t know.

Be careful in this time.
Not all exits lead out.

releases.credits:

releases.description:
In contrast to Muzak, often understood as background music aimed at influencing consumer behavior, MuDak could be described as "active music" or "engaging music." From a musicological perspective, music that demands attention and puts listeners at the center of the experience could be seen as the opposite of Muzak.

Such music is often more complex, requires deeper engagement, and encourages emotional responses or critical thinking. Examples include “Neue Musik”, jazz improvisations, or avant-garde music, where listeners must actively listen and engage with the music rather than just experiencing it as background noise. Another aspect is the use of music in a context that promotes cultural or social criticism, such as protest songs or politically motivated music that makes listeners think and mobilize.

#Berlin #Cologne

releases.title: A Practical Guide to Sound Cooking
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by D.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
A Practical Guide to Sound Cooking

Experimental Recipes for Crafting Unique Sonic Flavors
Introduction: Cooking with Sound

Sound artists and engineers are like sonic chefs, blending raw sonic ingredients, applying processing techniques, and "plating" sounds in immersive ways. Just like in cooking, the goal is to balance texture, depth, and contrast—sometimes through careful refinement and sometimes through controlled chaos.
Each of the following "recipes" focuses on a different approach to sound creation: acoustic manipulation, synthesis, and digital sculpting.

Recipe 1: The Bubbling Monolith
A heavy, evolving, liquid-metal drone with dynamic movement
(Best for ambient, sci-fi, or horror sound design)
Ingredients:
 • Base Ingredient: Granular Synthesis of low-frequency bubbling sounds 
 • Seasoning: Reverb & Resonators 
 • Cooking Method: Layering & Time Manipulation 
Instructions:
 1. Collect the raw sound – Start with a recording of thick bubbling (boiling tar, deep water, or even a slowed-down carbonated drink fizz). 
 2. Slice and stretch – Use a granular synthesis engine (like PaulStretch or a granular sampler) to slow down the bubbles until they morph into deep, pulsating textures. 
 3. Marinate with resonance – Run the stretched audio through a resonator (like Ableton’s Corpus or a tuned reverb) to emphasize specific harmonics, making it feel metallic and cavernous. 
 4. Garnish with motion – Apply slow LFO modulation to pitch and filtering to create subtle movement, as if the sound is shifting between physical states. 
Serving Suggestion:
Use this as an eerie atmospheric bed for film soundtracks, or layer it under dialogue to add a subconscious sense of unease.

Recipe 2: Electric Ghost Soup
A chaotic yet controlled interplay of glitchy textures and evolving harmonics
(Great for experimental electronic music and game sound design)
Ingredients:
 • Base Ingredient: Contact microphones on metallic objects 
 • Seasoning: FM Synthesis & Granular Scrambling 
 • Cooking Method: Feedback Loops 
Instructions:
 1. Prepare the broth – Attach contact mics to vibrating surfaces like sheet metal or springs. Record the resonances from tapping, scraping, or bowing the object. 
 2. Blend in the synthesis – Use FM synthesis (like Yamaha DX7 or Operator in Ableton) to recreate the same tonal qualities found in your recorded metal sounds. 
 3. Chop and scramble – Use granular synthesis or FFT-based effects to break apart and reassemble the sound into glitchy, fragmented textures. 
 4. Add controlled chaos – Route the processed sound through a self-oscillating delay or feedback network (e.g., a no-input mixer setup) to introduce unexpected artifacts. 
 5. Taste and refine – Apply dynamic EQ or spectral morphing to shape the sound into something eerie yet musical. 
Serving Suggestion:
Perfect as a soundtrack for surrealist horror or to add depth to glitch and IDM music.

Recipe 3: The Elastic Chameleon
A morphing sound effect that shifts between organic and synthetic qualities
(Ideal for foley replacement, animated creatures, or shape-shifting entities in sound design.)
Ingredients:
 • Base Ingredient: Human Vocalizations + Synthesized Formants 
 • Seasoning: Convolution Reverb & Spectral Morphing 
 • Cooking Method: Hybrid Layering 
Instructions:
 1. Record raw vocal textures – Growls, whispers, throat noises, or exaggerated vowel sounds make a great foundation. 
 2. Synthesize a harmonic twin – Use a formant filter or additive synthesis (like Vocaloid, Morphoder, or a modular synth) to generate a synthetic counterpart. 
 3. Stir together with convolution reverb – Take an impulse response of an unusual space (e.g., a mic’d-up glass jar, a resonating metal tube, or underwater hydrophones) and apply it to the layered sound. 
 4. Stretch and bend – Use spectral morphing tools (like Zynaptiq Morph or IRCAM’s AudioSculpt) to seamlessly blend the organic and synthetic layers, making the sound constantly shift between recognizable and alien. 
 5. Refine the texture – Use transient shaping or reverse reverb to create swelling, ghostly movements. 
Serving Suggestion:
Use this for otherworldly creature sounds, transforming sci-fi machines, or even a mutating synthesizer patch in music production.

Recipe 4: Fermented Noise Caviar
A self-evolving, organic texture that grows richer and more unpredictable over time
(Perfect for evolving soundscapes, generative music, or deep background atmospheres in storytelling audio.)
Ingredients:
 • Base Ingredient: Field recordings of static noise, wind, or water 
 • Seasoning: Iterative reprocessing through tape loops or granular synthesis 
 • Cooking Method: Feedback Manipulation & Data Bending 
Instructions:
 1. Harvest the raw material – Record a dense yet natural sound source, like:
 ◦ Crackling leaves in the wind 
 ◦ A long exposure of city noise 
 ◦ A room tone with subtle air movement 
 ◦ The hum of an old power transformer 
 2. Begin the fermentation process – Process the recording through a low-fidelity medium:
 ◦ Run it through a reel-to-reel tape and re-record it at slower speeds. 
 ◦ Apply bit-reduction and dithering to degrade and emphasize hidden harmonics. 
 ◦ Convert it to RAW data and reimport it as audio to reveal digital artifacts. 
 3. Reintroduce the aged material – Layer the altered versions on top of the original recording, creating a feedback ecosystem. 
 4. Add spectral seasoning – Use spectral freeze or granular scattering to stretch and smear tiny details, emphasizing subtle micro-movements. 
 5. Final fermentation – Set up an auto-generative system (e.g., a Max/MSP patch or a Reaktor ensemble) to let the sound continuously process itself in a slow feedback loop over hours or days. 
Serving Suggestion:
 • Use as a self-regenerating background texture in immersive VR experiences. 
 • Layer under dialogue in an avant-garde film to create subconscious unease. 
 • Implement in modular synth performances for a constantly shifting sonic foundation. 

A Note on Sonic Fermentation
This approach mirrors the way fermentation in cooking enhances flavors over time. Letting sounds evolve through iterative processing—whether through tape loops, convolution processing, or digital resynthesis—creates unexpected textures that cannot be manually composed.

Recipe 5: Quantum Foam Reduction
A self-collapsing, ever-morphing texture that sounds like a black hole digesting time itself
(Best for extreme experimental music, horror soundscapes, or AI-driven sound synthesis.)
Ingredients:
 • Base Ingredient: AI-generated sound snippets or fragmented speech samples 
 • Seasoning: Spectral destruction & recursive resynthesis 
 • Cooking Method: Chaotic feedback systems 
Instructions:
 1. Generate unpredictable raw material – Use an AI voice model (like RVC or ElevenLabs) trained on a dataset of conflicting voices—try feeding it gibberish text or phonetic nonsense so it outputs bizarre, half-human utterances. 
 2. Shred it apart – Take the AI’s output and run it through:
 ◦ FFT-based spectral destruction (like Unfiltered Audio’s SpecOps) 
 ◦ Extreme phase vocoding (PaulXStretch pushed beyond recognition) 
 ◦ Convolution reverb using distorted impulse responses (use crushed metal sounds as IRs). 
 3. Feed it back into itself – Take the destroyed version and have another AI attempt to "reconstruct" it, forcing it to interpret the chaos as meaningful speech or music. 
 4. Inject randomness – Use modular synths or stochastic MIDI generators to control pitch/time shifts dynamically, ensuring every playback is slightly different. 
 5. Apply nonlinear reverb decay – Run the final sound through a self-modulating reverb with variable diffusion, making it decay in unpredictable, ghostly ways. 
Serving Suggestion:
 • Create nightmare-inducing ASMR with whispering, barely intelligible sounds that seem to be trying to "speak." 
 • Use as the voice of an alien intelligence in sci-fi media. 
 • Sample micro-fragments into granular synthesis for an ever-shifting rhythmic bed. 

Recipe 6: The Bioelectric Slime Choir
A living, breathing sonic organism built from biofeedback and neural synthesis.
(Ideal for AI-generated music, bio-sonification, or experimental live performances.)
Ingredients:
 • Base Ingredient: EEG brainwave or biosensor data converted into sound 
 • Seasoning: Neural network-based synthesis 
 • Cooking Method: Generative self-modulation 
Instructions:
 1. Capture raw biosignals – Use EEG headsets, EMG muscle sensors, or heart rate monitors to record fluctuations in biological signals. 
 2. Translate body data into MIDI/Sound – Route the biosignal data into a Max/MSP or Pure Data patch to control pitch, synthesis parameters, or sample triggering. 
 3. Apply neural synthesis – Use AI-based synthesizers (like Google’s NSynth or RAVE) to reinterpret your biosignals into fluid, alien vocalizations. 
 4. Introduce environmental influence – Implement real-time adaptive processing, where external stimuli (e.g., temperature, movement, or external audio input) alter the sonic output. 
 5. Allow it to evolve – Set up a self-generating patch where the system continuously learns and modifies its own behavior based on past performances. 
Serving Suggestion:
 • Turn your own brain activity into an evolving ambient drone piece. 
 • Use muscle tension to trigger sound effects in an experimental live performance. 
 • Create an "audio entity" that listens, learns, and responds in real time. 

Recipe 7: The Catastrophic Chain Reaction
A sound so unstable it literally consumes itself—perfect for chaotic noise performances and sonic destruction.
Ingredients:
 • Base Ingredient: No-input mixing feedback 
 • Seasoning: Dynamic overdrive & unpredictably mapped MIDI controllers 
 • Cooking Method: Recursive entropy loops 
Instructions:
 1. Create a no-input feedback system – Plug a mixing board’s output back into its input and manipulate EQ/gain to sculpt feedback tones. 
 2. Introduce reactive overdrive – Route the output into a distortion unit with envelope followers that increase distortion as the volume rises, forcing the sound to eat itself. 
 3. Automate decay failure – Use a compressor with an extreme ratio, but invert the attack/release mapping, causing the sound to unpredictably swell and vanish. 
 4. Add random control layers – Use a generative MIDI sequencer to dynamically alter effects chains at random intervals, so the system constantly reinvents itself. 
 5. Let it implode – Introduce a slow pitch-shifting delay with an infinitely rising or falling glissando (like Shepard tones) so the sound continuously spirals into oblivion. 
Serving Suggestion:
 • Use in live noise performances where the audience’s physical presence affects the feedback loop. 
 • Sample short bursts into a glitch-heavy techno track for controlled chaos. 
 • Record and slow it down 1,000% to create massive, apocalyptic drones. 

Beyond Cooking: Sound Alchemy
At this point, we’re not just "cooking" sounds—we’re creating living systems that react, evolve, and sometimes destroy themselves. These approaches embrace the unexpected, making every performance or rendering unique.
Final Notes: Becoming a Sonic Chef
Sound design, much like cooking, is about experimenting with textures, balancing ingredients, and refining techniques. These recipes are just a starting point—once you get comfortable, start "improvising" your own unique flavors by swapping out techniques, layering different base sounds, and exploring unpredictable effects.

releases.description: Arcadia represents an idyllic vision of pastoralism and harmony, an untouched wilderness where life mirrors the Edenic form of existence. In this timeless land, people continue to live as in the Golden Age, untouched by the pride and greed that taint other regions. It shares a deep connection to the Noble Savage, living close to nature and embodying virtue, free from the corruption of civilization. The land is filled with beautiful nymphs frolicking in lush forests, creating a paradise of peace. Here, the beauty of the grass outshines emeralds, and the voices of nature resonate in a harmonious chorus. Does not the serenity of this place offer enough reward for any time lost or dangers faced, with every moment spent in perfect unity? One would be loath to leave such a place, lamenting the departure from a world so pure and harmonious.

releases.title: The Solar Lodge
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits: Produced by M
releases.description:
In the foggy streets of London in the 1920s, at a time when the occult and the mystical captured the imagination of many, a secret circle known as the Solar Lodge existed. This lodge was a meeting place for artists, thinkers, and occultists dedicated to exploring human consciousness and magic. Among them was one particularly remarkable man: Austin Osman Spare, a visionary artist and mystic.

The Solar Lodge was a place of inspiration and exchange, where the boundaries between reality and dream were blurred. In the steamy rooms of the lodge, members met regularly to share their ideas and beliefs. Spare, known for his extraordinary drawings and unconventional approach to magic, was a fervent advocate of individual experience and self-realization.

One evening, as a golden full moon shone through the lodge windows, the members gathered for a special ceremony. Spare had a vision: he wanted to manifest the powers of the unconscious and guide the participants into a deeper connection with their own creativity. He believed that every person had the ability to unlock their inner magic and that this could be done through connecting with the symbols and archetypes that lived in their dreams.

The ceremony began with the drawing of symbols drawn from the collective unconscious. Spare led those present into a trance-like meditation where they could explore their own inner images and visions. During this time, the lodge members felt a collective energy pulsing through the room, as if the walls themselves were coming to life.

As the ceremony reached its climax, Spare invited those present to find their own artistic expressions. Some painted, others wrote poetry, while still others experimented with music. Spare himself drew a bizarre, dreamlike scene depicting a fusion of humanity and nature—a metaphor for the unity of self with the universe.

In the following weeks and months, the Solar Lodge gained increasing attention. Members became well-known in London's artistic and spiritual community, and Spare's works gained recognition for their unique blend of surrealism and occultism. The Lodge became a catalyst for creative expression, and many who were initially skeptical soon found themselves captivated.

But with growing notoriety came outside attention. Some people viewed the Lodge as a threat, others as a curiosity. Rumors and misunderstandings abounded about the members' practices, and soon the atmosphere of the Lodge became pervaded by tension and anxiety.

Austin Osman Spare, who so highly valued creative freedom and the individual pursuit of truth, realized that the Lodge had to change to survive. In a final, powerful act, he called the members together and suggested that they close the lodge and take their ideas and practices out into the world. It was time to bring the light of creativity and magic into people's hearts, rather than hiding in the shadows of secrecy.

So it was that the Solar Lodge closed its doors, but its teachings and inspirations were carried out into the world. Austin Osman Spare and his companions became ambassadors of a new era of creativity, in which the occult and the artistic were intertwined. Their visions lived on in the works they left behind and in the hearts of the people they touched.

And so the memory of the Solar Lodge and Austin Osman Spare remains alive, a shining example of how the creative spirit and the search for truth can transcend the boundaries of the ordinary.

releases.title: Farewell
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits: Produced by K
releases.description:
Today, with a heavy heart, I write these words to you — my steadfast companion through countless mornings, long nights, and quiet afternoons. You were a piece of history, a silent witness molded from the remnants of a world forever changed. Born from the steel of a helmet after the war, you carried with you the resilience and the strange poetry of survival.
Since my earliest memories, you have been by my side — chipped, weathered, but never broken. You held not just coffee, but warmth, comfort, and the rituals of my life. In your simple, battered form, you taught me that beauty lies not in polish or perfection, but in the stories, we carry and the journeys we endure.
You have felt my tired hands at dawn, my hopeful ones at new beginnings, my trembling ones at moments of loss. Together, we laughed, we dreamed, we grew older.
Today, it is time to let you rest. Your metal, once strong enough to shield a soldier, now bears the gentle scars of a life well-lived. It would be selfish to ask more of you when you have already given so much.
Thank you, my old friend, for every sip, every shared moment, every unspoken comfort. You will always be part of my story — and I will always carry you, somewhere, quietly, within me.
Farewell

releases.title: Fox on the Run
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits: Produced by M
releases.description:
In an interwoven, endless forest of shadows and whispering mists lived a small fox named Finn. His eyes were like luminous fragments, but his mind was heavy with questions gathering in the dark corners of his consciousness. He searched for the meaning of his existence, but the answers seemed like fading shadows, constantly dissolving into the mists.

Every day, Finn wandered through the tangled corridors of trees that seemed like living beings watching him. He wondered what would make him truly happy, but the world only answered with whispering voices that drove him mad.

One evening, when the world shimmered in unnatural colors and time itself seemed to melt away, Finn encountered a wise owl named Luna. She soared through the air, her wings like broken mirrors, her eyes deep, unfathomable abysses. She spoke in riddles: "Meaning is only an illusion, a shadow dancing in the fog. You search for it in vain."

Finn felt her words shatter like shimmering crystals in his mind, catapulting him into another reality. Together they flew through the surreal, constantly changing forest—trees that grew into the sky and sank back into the ground, rivers that flowed backward, and shadows that came to life.

But one morning, as the sun rose an unnatural violet, Luna vanished without a trace. Finn was left behind, surrounded by whispering shadows and shimmering dust that hung in the air like tears. The pain was like an endless abyss swallowing him up, with no hope of return.

In that moment, Finn realized that the search for meaning was only a delusion—an illusion that was driving him mad. The memories of Luna and the dreams they had shared shattered like glass and scattered into the endless mists.

Finn continued running, lost in the darkness, without a destination, without hope. The forest around him was no longer a place of understanding, but a labyrinth of shadows and memories that drew him ever deeper into the void.

And so the little fox disappeared into the endless mists, a lost shadow searching for something that never existed.

releases.title: Jam Session | Montreux (1972)
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits: Produced by K
releases.description:
MDK 091 - Jam Session | Montreux (1972) (Polycam / Synthetic Harvest)

Review by Lucien Renard | Actuel, March 1973
If electricity could dream, its dreams might sound like this.
Little is known about MDK — no full names, no liner credits, not even a photograph. Just a stark, grainy sleeve: concentric circles like radio pulses over a silhouette of Lake Geneva. 
The music inside is more mysterious still. MDK - Jam Session | Montreux (1972) is not jazz, nor rock, nor musique concrète. And yet, it pulls threads from all three — then stretches them into shapes that shouldn’t exist.

Side A begins with "White Sugar", an icy cascade of electronic textures — imagine Neu! reinterpreting Miles Davis' Bitches Brew with circuitry instead of brass. It bleeds into "Influence", where the rhythm section dissolves and reforms in waves, punctuated by glissandi and what might be tape loops of a man whispering in Russian.
"Bribed Horses" is the first of several moments where MDK seems to be building music not with notes, but with atmosphere. Hoof-like percussion, detuned organ stabs, and string pads that sound more like insect wings than anything orchestral. It’s disconcerting, and hypnotic.
But nothing prepares the listener for "Can’t You Bear Me Flocking" — a seven-minute centrepiece that veers into avant-garde opera, noise collage, and what I can only describe as dystopian gospel. The vocalist (or is it a vocoder?) pleads and loops phrases like "bear me flocking" until they lose all meaning. It is terrifying and beautiful.

Side B begins more tenderly with "You Moved", a track that sounds like a lullaby composed on a malfunctioning Mellotron. "Mother" follows — halfway between lullaby and lament, as if mourning something that hasn't yet happened.
Then comes "Sticky Blues", the most “traditional” piece — a 12-bar frame draped in buzzing synths and low-end growls. It’s boogie-woogie through an oscilloscope. "Brother OxyContin" is a standout: a heavy, plodding track that feels almost prophetic in its evocation of pharmaceutical sorrow. The title alone will be meaningless to most in ’73, but somehow it feels tragically familiar.
"Rainbow Flowers" and "Moonlight Slipslop" close the set with eerie optimism. The former channels early Floyd pastoralism, but filtered through synthetic dew. The latter — all gliding filters and delayed keys — sounds like moonlight reflecting on a machine’s eye.

Verdict: ★★★★★
MDK - Jam Session | Montreux (1972) is not just ahead of its time — it seems unaware that time exists at all. MDK may vanish tomorrow, or they may be broadcasting from the future. Either way, this document remains: a strange, haunting record of a night when the future slipped through the curtain.

3 by 3
Three musicians, three souls connected across space and time, elevating the vibrations of the universe with the purest expression of humanity: culture and music.
Three audiences in three corners of the world will witness a unique event – a sonic ritual on three stages, in three different times, united by a single energy.
The number 3 is not magical. No number is on its own. But in its repetition, in its rhythm, in its hidden dance with fate, it intertwines with the harmonies and resonances of the whole. 3 is just a number... until it becomes a portal.
The music that emerges from this session is the echo of three stories, three cultures: a German breathing the air of his homeland, another moving between Mexico City and Tlaxcala, and a Mexican feeling the heartbeat of Cuautla. Their roots intertwine, their sounds merge, their message transcends borders.
We sail through seas of frequencies to cleanse the shadows spreading through the networks, to dissolve the fear woven from threads of deception. We create sound to dismantle lies, to transform panic into awareness.
We explore impossible harmonies, traverse rhythmic paths of 7/4 that eventually find their destination in 4/4. In every measure, in every note, we seek to express the pain that some humans inflict upon others – but also the spark of joy that can illuminate even the darkest night. We cling to the present, dance with the audience beyond borders and identities, striving to survive in a world of shadows and light.
We sing of love as an act of resistance. We paint with sound visions of paradise and the apocalypse. We want to save the world, but our only tools are waves, rhythms, and soundscapes.
For a moment, with a chord, with a single vibration, we can redraw reality for those who listen. We will attempt it now – in 3... in three places, in a single moment.
Join the journey.

The number of summers (or years) a bird lives depends heavily on the species. Here's a general 
breakdown:
Small songbirds (e.g., sparrows, finches):
 • Lifespan: 2–5 years on average in the wild
 • Some individuals can live up to 10 years or more, but most die young due to predation, disease, or accidents.
Medium-sized birds (e.g., pigeons, crows):
 • Lifespan: 5–15 years
 • Crows can live 20+ years in the wild and even longer in captivity.
Large birds (e.g., hawks, eagles, parrots):
 • Lifespan: 15–50 years
 • Parrots especially are known for long lives — some live 60+ years in captivity.
 • Albatrosses and other seabirds can also reach 40–60 years.

releases.title: Cry me a river
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by M.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
In a city where the sun never fully shone and the clouds always weighed heavily on the rooftops, lived a society of people who had lost themselves in an endless whirlpool of discontent. They were masters at denying their own faults, blaming others, and questioning everything—even their own existence.

Every night, when the streets were empty and only the gentle whisper of the wind moved through the alleys, they gathered by the river. A river so deep that it swallowed the truth before it could ever reach its shore. Here, on the banks of "Cry Me a River," they wept their endless tears. Not because they could feel, but because they believed their tears could change the world—yet they only changed the surface of the water, which always showed the same dark reflections.

They were convinced that the world's faults lay with the minorities—with those who were different, who didn't conform, who lived their own truth. They blamed them while remaining stuck in their own inability to take responsibility. They constantly asked themselves, "Who is to blame?" But never themselves. Instead, they questioned everything—reality, history, the truth. They denied the obvious, because why not? The unknown was easier to ignore than their own failures.

In this world, there were no clear answers. Only constant denial, twisting, and clinging to illusions. They believed the river was taking their tears, but in truth, they were only drinking the water of despair that they themselves had poured into the river. The river was a mirror to their souls: deep, black, and full of unfulfilled longings.

One day, as they stood by the river again, weeping their tears, the water began to rise. It became restless, as if telling them it had had enough. But they heard only the echo of their own voices, questioning everything—except their own discontent.

And so the tears continued to flow, incessantly, in an endless stream. A river that absorbed everything—the guilt, the doubt, the fear—and never gave any of it back. A tragically surreal place where people themselves were the architects of their own suffering, trapped in a perpetual cycle of self-deception and denial.

releases.description:
What & How

Tell me what you want to say,
send me words, as sharp as light.
But there’s more than just the phrases
flickering across your face at night.

In between the lines, things bloom—
unspoken shadows, crisp and clear.
You say: “Everything’s just fine,”
and I hear it, yes, I hear.

Because there’s more than just the sound,
more than lyrics in your song.
The way you say it shifts the ground—
betrays what’s right and feels what´s wrong.

What you say – is just the one part,
but how you mean it moves the heart.
Between the facts, between the lines,
lives the code that holds or breaks our ties.
The message may whisper – but the meaning shouts.
What & How – you say and mean it.

You tell me that all is fine,
but there's a hesitation in your tone.
Like a shadow cast on time,
like a fog between each word you've thrown.
Your WhatsApp read all polite,
but the rain was in your phrasing.
The content said it all just right –
but you were done with even saying.

Because there’s more than just the text,
more than neat and proper flow.
How we stand, how we connect –
can't be shaped by words alone.

What you say – is just one part,
but how you mean it moves the heart.
Between the facts, between the lines,
lives the code that holds or breaks our ties.
The message may whisper – but the meaning shouts.
What & How – you say and mean it.

Don’t just speak straight from your gut,
I hear what's floating in between.
Every “yes” with eyes wide shut –
breaks more than a single misstep could mean.

What you say – sounds clear, composed,
but how you mean it makes it close.
In the silence, tone, and glance –
lives the thread that gives us one last chance.
What & How – you say and mean it.

//////////////////////////////////////



Was & Wie

Sag mir, was du sagen willst,
schick mir Worte, scharf wie Licht.
Doch da flackern mehr als Sätze,
irgendwas in deinem Gesicht.
Zwischen Zeilen blühen Schatten,
ungesagt und dennoch klar.
Du sagst: „Alles ist in Ordnung“,
und ich hör, es war mal wahr.

Denn da ist mehr als nur der Klang,
mehr als Text in deinem Lied.
Wie du’s sagst, lässt Welten kippen,
verrät dich und das was dich trieb.

Was du sagst – ist nur ein Teil,
doch wie du’s meinst, trägt uns erst weiter.
Zwischen Fakten, zwischen Zeilen,
liegt der Code, der uns verbindet und zerreißt.
Der Inhalt kann leise sein – doch die Beziehung schreit.
Was & Wie, du sagst und meinst.

Sagst du mir, dass alles läuft, 
in deiner Stimme liegt ein Zögern. 
Wie ein Schatten auf der Zeit, 
wie ein Nebel zwischen Wörtern.

In WhatsApp war’s ganz korrekt,
doch fühlte sich an wie Regen.
Der ganze Inhalt war komplett –
doch du wolltest nicht mehr reden.

Denn da ist mehr als nur der Text,
mehr als Ordnung, glatt und klar.
Wie wir stehen, wie wir fühlen –
machst du nicht durch Sprache wahr.

Was du sagst – ist nur ein Teil,
doch wie du’s meinst, trägt uns erst weiter.
Zwischen Fakten, zwischen Zeilen,
liegt der Code, der uns verbindet und zerreißt.
Der Inhalt kann leise sein – doch die Beziehung schreit.
Was & Wie, du sagst und meinst.

Sprich nicht nur aus deinem Bauch,
ich höre auch, was zwischen deinen Worten schwebt.
Jedes „Ja“ mit falschem Blick –
macht mehr kaputt als jedes Missgeschick.

Was du sagst – klingt logisch, klar.
Doch wie du es meinst, macht es erst wahr.
Zwischen Zeilen, Ton und Blick –
liegt das Band, das uns hält oder zerreißt.
Was & Wie, du sagst und meinst.

////////////////////////////////////



Qué y Cómo

Dime qué me quieres decir,
lánzame palabras, luz cortante.
Pero hay más que solo frases,
algo vibra en tu semblante.

Entre líneas, florecen las cosas:
sombras silenciosas, nítidas y claras.
Dices: "Todo está bien",
y lo oigo, sí, lo oigo.

Porque hay más que solo el son,
más que letra en tu canción.
La forma en que lo dices conmueve,
traiciona lo correcto y siente lo incorrecto.

Lo que dices – es solo un trozo,
pero cómo lo dices, marca el paso.
Entre datos, entre frases,
vive el código que une o parte.
El mensaje puede ser suave – pero el lazo grita.
Qué y Cómo – lo dices y quieres decir.

Dices que todo va bien,
pero tu voz no está tan firme.
Como un velo sobre el tiempo,
como niebla entre los vistes.
Tu WhatsApp fue muy correcto,
pero frío como invierno.
El mensaje estaba claro –
pero tú, ya en otro cielo.

Porque hay más que solo texto,
más que forma sin error.
Lo que somos, lo que importa –
no se puede moldear solo con palabras.

Lo que dices – es solo un trozo,
pero cómo lo dices, marca el paso.
Entre datos, entre frases,
vive el código que une o parte.
El mensaje puede ser suave – pero el lazo grita.
Qué y Cómo – lo dices y quieres decir.

No hables solo desde el vientre,
escucho el eco entre tus frases.
Cada “sí” con ojos cerrados –
rompe más que mil desastres.

Lo que dices – suena preciso,
pero cómo lo dices, trae sentido.
En los gestos, en los tonos –
vive el hilo que nos une… o perdemos.
Qué y Cómo – lo dices y quieres decir.

releases.title: After Dark
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by M.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
Every night, at two o'clock sharp, the show began. No one knew which channel it was on. The frequency was untraceable, the screen displayed no logo, and no commercials interrupted the transmission. Yet it was always there: After Dark.

Gregor M., who had been working in a windowless office for six months, had discovered the show by chance. Or rather, the show had discovered him. It was one of those nights when he couldn't sleep because the silence was too loud. He turned on the television without looking at the screen, and suddenly he heard his name.

"Good evening, Gregor," said the host.

The man on the screen wore an immaculate suit that seemed to have come from another era. His face was smooth, too smooth, without any real features—as if it were a sketch, still unfinished. The camera hovered around him, but his gaze was always fixed directly on Gregor.

"Glad you're back on," the host continued. Gregor had never turned on before.

He wanted to turn off the television, but the remote control was missing. The power cord was unplugged – yet the picture continued to flicker. The sound was clearer than ever.

"Tonight we have a special guest," said the presenter, turning to a door at the edge of the studio. It opened silently.

Gregor saw himself enter.

The studio version of him was wearing the same clothes he was wearing at that moment – a gray shirt that was tight at the shoulders and worn trousers that he never liked but always wore. The copy sat down in the guest chair with a tired, almost guilty gesture.

"Mr. M., tell us: Why did you decide not to fill out the form completely back then?" The presenter smiled. It wasn't a human smile – too symmetrical, too empty.

Gregor felt his heart rate increase. He didn't remember a form. Or did he? There were so many.

"I... I don't know," his studio version replied.

The moderator nodded in understanding. "Knowledge is a deceptive thing, isn't it?"

Suddenly, the camera panned to the audience. It was empty. And yet Gregor thought he heard applause. A soft, hollow clapping that seemed to rise from a very deep, very dark pit.

The show continued. Old colleagues appeared, strangers Gregor had never seen, but whose voices sounded familiar. Everyone told stories about him—small deviations, false memories, subtle distortions. He became increasingly uncertain whether they might not be right after all. Had he really been there? Had he really said that?

The moderator smiled continuously.

"And finally, Mr. M.," he finally said, "may you ask yourself: What exactly were you supposed to do?"

Gregor couldn't answer.

He stared at the screen until the picture slowly faded to black. No credits, no music. Just darkness.

He looked at the clock. It was two o'clock.

The television was off.

He didn't know if the show would ever come back.

But every night, at two o'clock sharp, he waited for it.
Feedback geben

releases.title: In the wrong place at the right time
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits:
Produced by K.
Cover art by D.
releases.description:
Being somewhere — or rather, anywhere — when one is supposed to be here is a choreography I’ve never mastered. Time, of course, remains indifferent, perhaps even cruel, as it always has been — not quite an enemy, but certainly never a companion. 
And yet now, with you, I’m almost certain — dangerously certain — that we are precisely where we shouldn't be. Perhaps the hour itself is accurate, aligned even, though still inhospitable.
Your voice drips through the static air, and I catch traces of your perfume — or maybe just the memory of it — suspended like something almost tasty. Still, this space, this here, is not yours. I see it. It doesn’t want you, and you don’t want it. Nothing fits.
So, dream of me — vividly, desperately — but elsewhere. Dream of an elsewhere that dreams you back. 
But remember: Time has rarely taken sides, and if it did, it never took ours.

releases.title: Autumn will come
releases.creator: MDK
releases.credits: Produced by M
releases.description:
The clocks refused to move forward.

For days, the sky hung in a silent breath between summer heat and the first, cool whisper of the mist. The trees, uncertain of their own existence, held their leaves back like playing cards, as if waiting for a sign. And then it came—not as a storm, not as rain, but as light.

It was the light of autumn, suddenly appearing in the city like a forgotten memory. It came not from the sun, but from the windows of houses, from the cracks in old walls, from the eyes of stray cats. It was the color of amber, mingled with the taste of rusty iron and warm cider.

Mr. Grau, a clockmaker without time, stepped out of his shop and looked up.
"Autumn will come," he said to no one.
His shadow answered, "It is already here."

On the rooftops, crows began to whisper in ancient languages. Her wings cast patterns on the pavement that looked like the notes of a forgotten symphony. The children of the city heard her and danced backward. Her footsteps left golden echoes that didn't fade away, but condensed into spirals in the air.

A woman with a face like a smooth pear slice entered the park. Behind her grew a tree that bore no leaves, but small, glowing bulbs. Every time the wind blew, they flickered, as if remembering a summer that never came.

"Why is the light so soft?" asked a child with three mouths.
"Because it falls through memories," answered the pear woman. "And memories have no edges."

In the old cinema, a film played without a projector. The screen showed only shadows, but everyone watching recognized themselves—standing in the autumn light, somewhere, sometime, maybe yesterday, maybe next year.

In an alley, the leaves were made of paper. On each leaf was a word. Those who read them began to speak in verse and could never lie again. Some whispered poems, others remained silent forever.

Autumn didn't come—it simply was.

A state, a light, a quiet upheaval in the flesh of things.

And when night fell, it wasn't dark, but bronze. The stars smelled of cinnamon. Someone placed a cup of tea on the windowsill of a house that was no longer there.
